name: CARScenes v1
description: Annotation-only release for the CARScenes severity-aware scene-understanding benchmark with fully human-corrected labels.
citeAs: CARScenes authors. CARScenes v1: Severity-aware structured scene understanding benchmark. 2026.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: VLM-assisted labeling, human correction, and CARScenes release canonicalization
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Initial labels were generated by a VLM-assisted first pass, then every released record was human-corrected and normalized by the release builder.

distribution.name: carscenes_v1_records.jsonl
distribution.description: Canonical CARScenes v1 annotation records in JSONL format.

distribution.name: carscenes_v1_schema.json
distribution.description: Machine-readable schema for the benchmark fields.

distribution.name: dev-500.jsonl
distribution.description: Development split manifest for model selection.

distribution.name: gold-test-100.jsonl
distribution.description: Held-out benchmark split used for headline evaluation.

distribution.name: gold-agreement-25.jsonl
distribution.description: Independent agreement subset drawn from the held-out benchmark split.

distribution.name: LICENSES.md
distribution.description: License matrix for CARScenes release artifacts and upstream sources.

rai:dataCollection: CARScenes v1 canonicalizes scene-level annotations for front-camera frames drawn from Argoverse1, Cityscapes, KITTI, and nuScenes. Initial labels were generated by a VLM-assisted first pass and then reviewed and corrected by a human annotator for every released record.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: CARScenes uses versioned benchmark releases. The published v1 split manifests, schema, and evaluator are frozen and future revisions will be released under new versions rather than modifying v1 in place.
rai:dataPreprocessing: The release builder aligns image and label basenames, canonicalizes enum drift, repairs misplaced fields, removes wrapper records, emits a stable schema, and logs unmatched items before release.
rai:dataUseCases: Structured scene-understanding evaluation for driving VLMs
Cross-dataset querying with a common schema
rai:dataUses: Structured scene-understanding evaluation for driving VLMs
Cross-dataset querying with a common schema
rai:dataSocialImpact: CARScenes can support more transparent evaluation of driving-scene understanding by localizing failures under fixed contextual slices. It should not be used as evidence of crash risk, policy safety, deployment readiness, or correct driving action, because the release is single-frame, annotation-only, and severity is annotator-rated contextual difficulty.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: CARScenes does not add direct personal identifiers. The public release is annotation-only and does not redistribute source imagery.
rai:annotationsPerItem: One fully human-corrected canonical annotation record is released per frame. A 25-record subset also includes an independent second-pass review for agreement analysis.
rai:annotationProcedure: Initial labels are produced with a VLM-assisted first pass and then manually corrected field-by-field by a human annotator. A second reviewer independently re-annotates the gold-agreement-25 subset.
rai:annotationAnalysis: The independent second-pass review showed the strongest stability on directly observable fields such as road conditions, time of day, camera condition, traffic-light state, and overall visibility. Severity remained the most subjective label because it captures scene-level difficulty rather than a purely visual fact.
rai:machineAnnotationTools: A VLM-assisted annotation pipeline was used for first-pass labeling before full human correction.
rai:dataBiases: The release inherits source-dataset frequencies from the underlying corpora. Daytime scenes dominate the full corpus, high-severity scenes form a smaller slice, and source composition is uneven before balanced dev and gold split construction.
rai:dataLimitations: CARScenes is a single-frame, annotation-only benchmark. It does not redistribute upstream images, and interpretive fields such as ego maneuver and severity remain less stable than directly observable fields.
